lines.gurmukhi: ਲਿਖਿਆ ਪਲੈ ਪਾਇ ਸੋ ਸਚੁ ਜਾਣੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: likhiaa palai paae so sach jaaneeai |
lines.english: One whose pre-ordained destiny is activated, comes to know the True Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਉਜਲ ਤਾਰਣਹਾਰੁ ਸਬਦਿ ਪਛਾਣੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: bhaujal taaranahaar sabad pachhaaneeai |
lines.english: Realize the Word of the Shabad, and cross over the terrifying world-ocean.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੁਕਮੀ ਹੋਇ ਨਿਬੇੜੁ ਗਇਆ ਜਾਣੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: hukamee hoe niberr geaa jaaneeai |
lines.english: By God's Command, it is ordained. When the mortal goes, he knows.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਚਿ ਸਮਾਇ ਸੁ ਦਰਗਹ ਜਾਣੀਐ ॥੨੧॥
lines.translit: guramukh sach samaae su daragah jaaneeai |21|
lines.english: The Gurmukh is absorbed in the True Lord, and is famous in the Court of the Lord. ||21||

lines.gurmukhi: ਚੋਰ ਜਾਰ ਜੂਆਰ ਪੀੜੇ ਘਾਣੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: chor jaar jooaar peerre ghaaneeai |
lines.english: Thieves, adulterers and gamblers are pressed like seeds in the mill.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਿੰਦਕ ਲਾਇਤਬਾਰ ਮਿਲੇ ਹੜੑਵਾਣੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: nindak laaeitabaar mile harravaaneeai |
lines.english: Slanderers and gossipers are hand-cuffed.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਉ ਫਕੀਰੈ ਪਾਤਿਸਾਹੁ ਮੂਰਖ ਪੰਡਿਤੁ ਨਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: naau fakeerai paatisaahu moorakh panddit naau |
lines.english: The beggar is known as an emperor, and the fool is known as a religious scholar.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਧੇ ਕਾ ਨਾਉ ਪਾਰਖੂ ਏਵੈ ਕਰੇ ਗੁਆਉ ॥
lines.translit: andhe kaa naau paarakhoo evai kare guaau |
lines.english: The blind man is known as a seer; this is how people talk.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੨ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 2 |
lines.english: Salok, Second Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਲਤਿ ਕਾ ਨਾਉ ਚਉਧਰੀ ਕੂੜੀ ਪੂਰੇ ਥਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: eilat kaa naau chaudharee koorree poore thaau |
lines.english: The trouble-maker is called a leader, and the liar is seated with honor.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਜਾਣੀਐ ਕਲਿ ਕਾ ਏਹੁ ਨਿਆਉ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak guramukh jaaneeai kal kaa ehu niaau |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, the Gurmukhs know that this is justice in the Dark Age of Kali Yuga. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਣਾਂ ਬਾਜਾਂ ਤੈ ਸਿਕਦਾਰਾਂ ਏਨੑਾ ਪੜੑਿਆ ਨਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: haranaan baajaan tai sikadaaraan enaa parraiaa naau |
lines.english: Deer, falcons and government officials are known to be trained and clever.

lines.translit: mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਾਂਧੀ ਲਗੀ ਜਾਤਿ ਫਹਾਇਨਿ ਅਗੈ ਨਾਹੀ ਥਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: faandhee lagee jaat fahaaein agai naahee thaau |
lines.english: When the trap is set, they trap their own kind; hereafter they will find no place of rest.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਹਿਲੋ ਦੇ ਜੜ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਜੰਮੈ ਤਾ ਉਪਰਿ ਹੋਵੈ ਛਾਂਉ ॥
lines.translit: pahilo de jarr andar jamai taa upar hovai chhaanau |
lines.english: First, the tree puts down its roots, and then it spreads out its shade above.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਪੜਿਆ ਸੋ ਪੰਡਿਤੁ ਬੀਨਾ ਜਿਨੑੀ ਕਮਾਣਾ ਨਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: so parriaa so panddit beenaa jinaee kamaanaa naau |
lines.english: He alone is learned and wise, and he alone is a scholar, who practices the Name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਜੇ ਸੀਹ ਮੁਕਦਮ ਕੁਤੇ ॥
lines.translit: raaje seeh mukadam kute |
lines.english: The kings are tigers, and their officials are dogs;

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾਇ ਜਗਾਇਨੑਿ ਬੈਠੇ ਸੁਤੇ ॥
lines.translit: jaae jagaaeina baitthe sute |
lines.english: they go out and awaken the sleeping people to harass them.

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਾਕਰ ਨਹਦਾ ਪਾਇਨੑਿ ਘਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: chaakar nahadaa paaeina ghaau |
lines.english: The public servants inflict wounds with their nails.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਕਂ‍ੀ ਵਢਂ‍ੀ ਲਾਇਤਬਾਰ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: nakanee vadtanee laaeitabaar |2|
lines.english: Those who have violated the people's trust will be disgraced; their noses will be cut off. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਥੈ ਜੀਆਂ ਹੋਸੀ ਸਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: jithai jeean hosee saar |
lines.english: But there, in the Court of the Lord, all beings will be judged.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਤੁ ਪਿਤੁ ਕੁਤਿਹੋ ਚਟਿ ਜਾਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: rat pit kutiho chatt jaahu |
lines.english: The dogs lick up the blood that is spilled.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਤੇ ਭਉ ਊਪਜੈ ਪਾਈਐ ਮੋਖ ਦੁਆਰ ॥
lines.translit: satigur te bhau aoopajai paaeeai mokh duaar |
lines.english: Through the True Guru, the Fear of God wells up, and the Door of Salvation is found.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਿ ਉਪਾਏ ਮੇਦਨੀ ਆਪੇ ਕਰਦਾ ਸਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: aap upaae medanee aape karadaa saar |
lines.english: He Himself creates the world, and He himself takes care of it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੈ ਬਿਨੁ ਭਰਮੁ ਨ ਕਟੀਐ ਨਾਮਿ ਨ ਲਗੈ ਪਿਆਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: bhai bin bharam na katteeai naam na lagai piaar |
lines.english: Without the Fear of God, doubt is not dispelled, and love for the Name is not embraced.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੈ ਤੇ ਸਹਜੁ ਪਾਈਐ ਮਿਲਿ ਜੋਤੀ ਜੋਤਿ ਅਪਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: bhai te sehaj paaeeai mil jotee jot apaar |
lines.english: Through the Fear of God, intuitive ease is obtained, and one's light merges into the Light of the Infinite.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੈ ਤੇ ਭੈਜਲੁ ਲੰਘੀਐ ਗੁਰਮਤੀ ਵੀਚਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: bhai te bhaijal langheeai guramatee veechaar |
lines.english: Through the Fear of God, the terrifying world-ocean is crossed over, reflecting on the Guru's Teachings.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੈ ਤੇ ਨਿਰਭਉ ਪਾਈਐ ਜਿਸ ਦਾ ਅੰਤੁ ਨ ਪਾਰਾਵਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: bhai te nirbhau paaeeai jis daa ant na paaraavaar |
lines.english: Through the Fear of God, the Fearless Lord is found; He has no end or limitation.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖ ਭੈ ਕੀ ਸਾਰ ਨ ਜਾਣਨੀ ਤ੍ਰਿਸਨਾ ਜਲਤੇ ਕਰਹਿ ਪੁਕਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: manamukh bhai kee saar na jaananee trisanaa jalate kareh pukaar |
lines.english: The self-willed manmukhs do not appreciate the value of the Fear of God. Burning in desire, they weep and wail.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Salok, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਵੈ ਹੀ ਤੇ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਗੁਰਮਤੀ ਉਰਿ ਧਾਰ ॥੨੨॥
lines.translit: naanak naavai hee te sukh paaeaa guramatee ur dhaar |22|
lines.english: O Nanak, through the Name, peace is obtained, by enshrining the Guru's Teachings within the heart. ||22||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰੂਪੈ ਕਾਮੈ ਦੋਸਤੀ ਭੁਖੈ ਸਾਦੈ ਗੰਢੁ ॥
lines.translit: roopai kaamai dosatee bhukhai saadai gandt |
lines.english: Beauty and sexual desire are friends; hunger and tasty food are tied together.

lines.gurmukhi: ਲਬੈ ਮਾਲੈ ਘੁਲਿ ਮਿਲਿ ਮਿਚਲਿ ਊਂਘੈ ਸਉੜਿ ਪਲੰਘੁ ॥
lines.translit: labai maalai ghul mil michal aoonghai saurr palangh |
lines.english: Greed is bound up in its search for wealth, and sleep will use even a tiny space as a bed.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੰਉਕੈ ਕੋਪੁ ਖੁਆਰੁ ਹੋਇ ਫਕੜੁ ਪਿਟੇ ਅੰਧੁ ॥
lines.translit: bhnaukai kop khuaar hoe fakarr pitte andh |
lines.english: Anger barks and brings ruin on itself, blindly pursuing useless conflicts.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਜੁ ਮਾਲੁ ਰੂਪੁ ਜਾਤਿ ਜੋਬਨੁ ਪੰਜੇ ਠਗ ॥
lines.translit: raaj maal roop jaat joban panje tthag |
lines.english: Royal power, wealth, beauty, social status and youth are the five thieves.

lines.gurmukhi: ਚੁਪੈ ਚੰਗਾ ਨਾਨਕਾ ਵਿਣੁ ਨਾਵੈ ਮੁਹਿ ਗੰਧੁ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: chupai changaa naanakaa vin naavai muhi gandh |1|
lines.english: It is good to be silent, O Nanak; without the Name, one's mouth spews forth only filth. ||1||

lines.translit: mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਨਾ ਠਗਨੑਿ ਠਗ ਸੇ ਜਿ ਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਪੈਰੀ ਪਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: enaa tthagana tthag se ji gur kee pairee paeh |
lines.english: But these thieves themselves are robbed, by those who fall at the Guru's Feet.

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਨੀ ਠਗੀਂ ਜਗੁ ਠਗਿਆ ਕਿਨੈ ਨ ਰਖੀ ਲਜ ॥
lines.translit: enee tthageen jag tthagiaa kinai na rakhee laj |
lines.english: These thieves have plundered the world; no one's honor has been spared.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੜਿਆ ਲੇਖੇਦਾਰੁ ਲੇਖਾ ਮੰਗੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: parriaa lekhedaar lekhaa mangeeai |
lines.english: The learned and educated are called to account for their actions.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਕਰਮਾ ਬਾਹਰੇ ਹੋਰਿ ਕੇਤੇ ਮੁਠੇ ਜਾਹਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak karamaa baahare hor kete mutthe jaeh |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, the multitudes who do not have good karma are plundered. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚਾ ਵੇਪਰਵਾਹੁ ਸਬਦਿ ਸੰਤੋਖੀਆਂ ॥
lines.translit: sachaa veparavaahu sabad santokheean |
lines.english: Through the Shabad, the Word of the True and Independent Lord God, one becomes content.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਣੁ ਨਾਵੈ ਕੂੜਿਆਰੁ ਅਉਖਾ ਤੰਗੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: vin naavai koorriaar aaukhaa tangeeai |
lines.english: Without the Name, they are judged false; they become miserable and suffer hardship.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਉਘਟ ਰੁਧੇ ਰਾਹ ਗਲੀਆਂ ਰੋਕੀਆਂ ॥
lines.translit: aaughatt rudhe raah galeean rokeean |
lines.english: Their path becomes treacherous and difficult, and their way is blocked.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੁਹੇ ਮੁਹਿ ਚੋਟਾ ਖਾਹੁ ਵਿਣੁ ਗੁਰ ਕੋਇ ਨ ਛੁਟਸੀ ॥
lines.translit: muhe muhi chottaa khaahu vin gur koe na chhuttasee |
lines.english: Without the Guru, the mortals are beaten and punched in the face and the mouth, and no one is released.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਹਿਰ ਗਭੀਰ ਅਥਾਹੁ ਹਾਥ ਨ ਲਭਈ ॥
lines.translit: gahir gabheer athaahu haath na labhee |
lines.english: The Lord is deep and profound and unfathomable; His depth cannot be measured.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੁਕਮੀ ਸਾਹ ਗਿਰਾਹ ਦੇਂਦਾ ਜਾਣੀਐ ॥੨੩॥
lines.translit: hukamee saah giraah dendaa jaaneeai |23|
lines.english: Know that the Lord, by the Hukam of His Command, gives sustenance and the breath of life. ||23||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਤਿ ਸੇਤੀ ਘਰਿ ਜਾਹੁ ਨਾਮੁ ਵਖਾਣੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: pat setee ghar jaahu naam vakhaaneeai |
lines.english: Chanting the Naam, the Name of the Lord, one returns to his true home with honor.
